picks.match: Parramatta Eels vs Manly Warringah Sea Eagles
picks.reasoning: Manly sit at the bottom of the target odds range and represent the most defensible pick across all six matches today. The Sea Eagles have been the stronger NRL outfit this season, and Parramatta at home offers little deterrent given their inconsistent form. The news cycle around both clubs references NRLW content rather than any injury concerns or momentum shifts, so there's no credible evidence to move away from the market favourite here.

picks.match: St George Illawarra Dragons vs Wests Tigers
picks.reasoning: Dragons at home against the Tigers at 2.40 — that's genuine value right there. Wests Tigers are perennial underperformers and I can't back them as favourites in any derby, that just doesn't sit right. Dragons playing on their patch, bit of pride on the line, I'm taking them all day.

Australia at 3.85 at a World Cup knockout stage? Mate, I'm already booking the celebration. The Socceroos have history of punching above their weight on the biggest stage, and Egypt's odds suggest the market is sleeping on home continental support and Australia's never-say-die attitude. Nearly 4-to-1 for a team that belongs at this level? That's free money in a punter's language.
